content.feedback: Cartographer bootstrap at user/org scope ignores .leo/ boundaries in child repos. collectFiles walks the entire tree and dumps all drafts into the root memory/. Result: 230k docs in root instead of distributed across each repo's own memory/.
content.how_to_apply: collectFiles must detect directories with .leo/ and treat them as scope boundaries — skip those subtrees. Each repo should be bootstrapped independently via its own .leo/. Issue #77 tracks the fix. See also: cartographer generated docs with underscore tags (variable_declarator) violating kebab-case schema constraint — AST extractor tag sanitization needed.
content.why: User ran bootstrap from ~/Github (user scope) expecting each repo's memories to land in their respective .leo/memory/. Instead, all 230k docs (including 41k with invalid variable_declarator tags from AST extraction) landed in the root. Had to nuke the entire root memory/ and start over.
